mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in 60% or more of rate-cut periods, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a win rate above 55%, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production signals supply-side bottlenecks and reduced corporate output capacity, triggering short-term negative equity returns as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in 60% or more of INDPRO contraction periods, or the predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds the 12-month trailing inflation rate by more than 1.5 percentage points, real borrowing costs become restrictive, suppressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in over 50% of instances where the real rate threshold is breached, or the signal Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in 55% or more of positive payroll growth months, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and real personal consumption expenditures indicates weakening consumer demand relative to overall output, forecasting negative equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth exceeds RSAFS annualized growth by > 0.5%
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a win rate above 52%, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in industrial production and housing starts signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and construction sector strength, driving positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in 60% or more of joint expansion periods, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment exceeds 6%.
